items.headline: Tech Rally Drives S&P 500 and Nasdaq to New Records on Friday
items.summary: U.S. equities closed out the week on a strong note, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite achieving new all-time highs on Friday, April 24, 2026. A surge in technology and semiconductor stocks, fueled by strong earnings reports, propelled the market upward, overshadowing lingering geopolitical tensions and oil price concerns. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, however, experienced a slight dip.

items.headline: Intel Corp
items.price: Reported strong Q1 earnings and upbeat guidance.
items.summary: Intel's stock soared after beating Wall Street's first-quarter earnings forecasts and offering better-than-expected second-quarter guidance, especially on strong data center sales.

items.headline: Advanced Micro Devices
items.price: Driven by AI chip demand and positive analyst ratings.
items.summary: AMD shares surged following a price target raise by analysts and strong demand for AI chips.

items.headline: MaxLinear Inc
items.price: Upgraded on data center demand outlook.
items.summary: MaxLinear experienced a significant climb after Needham upgraded its stock rating due to data center demand.

items.headline: Charter Communications
items.price: Fell after Q1 earnings report, competition concerns.
items.summary: Charter Communications' stock declined following its earnings report and intensifying broadband competition concerns.

items.headline: Comcast Corporation
items.price: Broadband competition and stalled growth weighed on shares.
items.summary: Comcast shares plummeted due to intensifying broadband competition and concerns over stalled growth.

items.headline: HCA Healthcare, Inc.
items.summary: HCA Healthcare saw its stock fall during Friday's trading session.

items.headline: Technology and Energy Lead Market Performance
items.summary: For the week ending April 24, 2026, the technology sector continued its robust performance, driven by strong semiconductor and AI-related stocks. The energy sector also saw significant gains, rising 3.46%, despite fluctuating oil prices and geopolitical tensions. Information Technology and Financials also showed strong year-over-year earnings growth.

items.headline: Healthcare and Communication Services Lag
items.summary: Conversely, the healthcare sector was among the worst performers for the week, declining by 3.15%. Communication services also lagged, falling by 2.49%. Both sectors reported year-over-year declines in earnings.

items.headline: DOJ Drops Probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell
items.summary: The Department of Justice has dropped its criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell regarding allegations of false or misleading statements to Congress about the cost and scope of a Fed headquarters renovation. This development could smooth the way for Kevin Warsh's potential appointment to the Fed.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions and Oil Prices Remain a Factor
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions, particularly surrounding the Iran conflict, continued to influence market sentiment and oil prices throughout the week. The U.S. Department of the Treasury announced sanctions targeting entities involved in Iran's oil trade and shadow fleet. Oil prices, while seeing some intraday swings, were largely flat on Friday but remain elevated.

items.headline: Q1 2026 Earnings Season Shows Resilience
items.summary: The first-quarter 2026 earnings season is demonstrating resilience, with a higher-than-average percentage of S&P 500 companies reporting positive earnings surprises. Intel's strong earnings beat was a major market driver on Friday. Other notable companies like Procter & Gamble ($PG) and Union Pacific ($UNP) also reported earnings beats.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment at Record Lows Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: U.S. Consumer Sentiment for April registered a significant drop, hitting a record low, largely influenced by the ongoing Iran war and inflation expectations. The four-week moving average for initial unemployment claims increased slightly to 210,750.
